what_it_does: This file defines the frontend Application-Structure Pack: the architectural skeleton, component contracts, integration seams, token-slot styling interface, surface skeletons, and the recipe for adding regions/studio. It is written as the structural socket that a separate design system plugs into, not as a design system itself.
observations: It explicitly refuses to own aesthetic or build a design system; its role is to expose structure so Claude Design can produce design output that integrates cleanly.
It organizes the application into five layers: surface skeletons, component contracts, integration seams, token-slots, and conventions/laws.

It provides a token-slot contract with seven groups (surface tiers, signal, ink-on-bright, node kinds, typography, living instrument, shape) and five admitted gap categories (motion, breakpoints, z-index, opacity, icon sizing).
It lists roughly seventeen per-surface skeletons, each tagged with a status such as BUILT, DESIGNED, DESIGNED-☐, or INFERRED.
It states an interaction law: a click indicates and consents, but never executes; execution tier is resolved from the address union record's posture, not the verb.
It presents the Sequences primitive as a universal relational loop (resolve → present/work → persist → next/trigger) reused across engine, walkthrough, and cognition.
It gives a built-vs-net-new cut for the studio end-state, listing which backend primitives are already live and which binds remain unbuilt.
It includes a six-step recipe for adding a new region using kit primitives, useApp, addresses.json, parse.py, check.py, and gallery.py.
It describes the studio as Review.tsx, a full-screen view-level switch with three regions (Rail, Stage, RhmPanel) and five StudioKit components.
It notes that the design-lint gate check.py currently runs only on changed files during build dispatch and is not wired to CI, pre-commit hooks, or npm scripts.
It relies heavily on cross-references to BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md and the AUTHORING docs rather than restating their contents.
The file declares its own symbol list as empty.
standouts: The sockets-vs-plugs framing: structure owns the socket, Tim's design system owns the plug values.
The click-indicates-consent law as an architectural invariant that separates indication from execution.
The Sequences primitive treated as one relational loop instantiated at engine, walkthrough, and cognition scales.
The studio surface deliberately left as an unstyled structural scaffold for Claude Design to fill.
conventions: Cross-reference instead of restate: one-source discipline with BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md and AUTHORING docs.
Status taxonomy: BUILT, DESIGNED, DESIGNED-☐, INFERRED.
Every meaningful DOM element carries a data-ui-ref address.
Regions compose from five kit primitives and read state through useApp; they hold no domain state, effects, or fetch calls.
All styling resolves from CSS custom-property slots; hardcoded hex/px literals are contract violations.
Registry-is-truth: no hardcoded lists; mockups, addresses, and features are registry-driven.
Pending studio seams throw labelled errors rather than silently no-op.
Fail-loud behavior for corpus errors and POST failures.
concerns: The design-lint gate is acknowledged to be unenforced outside build dispatch, so token violations can be introduced silently.
A large portion of the surface skeletons and backend binds are marked DESIGNED-☐ or INFERRED, making the document partly aspirational.
Element-level addressing inside the sandboxed studio iframe is declared as an open, unsolved seam.
The token-slot contract has five admitted gap categories where values are still scattered as literals.
Heavy forward-referencing to BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md may make this file hard to read in isolation.
notes: A frontend architecture handoff document that defines the structural socket, component contracts, and surface skeletons a design system is meant to fill, with the studio left deliberately unstyled for Claude Design.
questions: What is the exact current content of BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md and the AUTHORING docs that this file repeatedly references?
Are the 45 registered ui:// addresses and 16 address_gap fields still current, or have they changed since 2026-06-08?
How will the pending studio seams (persistent locus, address-keyed tier, comment-to-intent promotion, in-frame deixis) be implemented?
What are the actual values in tokens.json and design-system.css today?
Is there a plan to wire check.py into CI, pre-commit hooks, or an npm lint script?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — it defines the structural socket, contracts, seams, token slots, surface skeletons, and recipes it claims to expose.
apparent_themes: Living instrument / presence as a functional design requirement
Sequences-as-universal-substrate across engine and human interaction
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a stable architectural socket that lets design and implementation proceed in parallel, while preventing the earlier mistake of duplicating backend primitives in a standalone studio or imposing an assistant-owned design system.
novelty: It treats a frontend architecture document as an explicit socket for a design system, with admitted token-slot gaps and a built/net-new status taxonomy, and it elevates a single interaction law and relational loop into architectural first-class concepts.
contribution: It contributes the structural framing, component contracts, surface skeleton taxonomy, token-slot interface, region-addition recipe, and studio integration brief that a fused system would keep as the design-to-code handoff contract.
summary_for_embedding: This file is the Application-Structure Pack, a frontend architecture document that defines the component contracts, surface skeletons, token-slot styling interface, integration seams, region-addition recipe, and studio brief intended as the structural socket into which Tim's design system plugs.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make the application's architecture visible and understandable to Claude Design so that design output lands in the right shape and integrates cleanly without the assistant imposing an aesthetic.
